insertions.translation_zh: In view of the continued challenges faced by the market, Hong Kong’s commercial real estate loan portfolio (excluding Mainland China loans) The quality of lenders' risk exposure) recorded adverse changes in the first half of 2025, mainly due to The quality of secured loans continues to deteriorate, with these loans accounting for 58% of the entire portfolio (December 31, 2024 “Substandard” and “impaired” loans increased to $8.5 billion (December 31, 2024 Date: US$8.2 billion), 94% of which are secured loans (December 31, 2024: 92%). As of June 30, 2025, the weighted average loan to valuation ratio is as follows: 45% of performing loans rated "below standard" (December 31, 2024: 46%); and record non-significant risk exposures with loan to valuation ratios higher than 70% (in 2024 December 31: US$1.2 billion); (12) "Credit impaired" loans were 67% (December 31, 2024: 58%). In combination The loan-to-value ratio for the $1.4 billion loan is above 70% (December 31, 2024: $1.2 billion Dollar). Collateral information and loan valuation ratios are calculated based on the total limit (including assets and liabilities Off-balance sheet commitments), as of June 30, 2025, was US$43.9 billion (December 31, 2024: 49.2 billion U.S. dollars). The size and quality of the unsecured loan portfolio remained stable, the level of "credit impaired" was limited, and 93% of ratings are "stable" or "good" (December 31, 2024: 93%). no compensation Mortgage loans are generally granted to stable Hong Kong-listed commercial real estate developers, which are usually The member companies of a comprehensive enterprise group have diversified cash flow. Although the residential real estate market has benefited from the easing of government restrictions, transaction volume since 2024 Remaining stable, but market conditions are expected to remain challenging. However, commercial real estate continues to face

Collateral information and loan valuation ratios are calculated based on the total limit (including assets and liabilities Off-balance sheet commitments), as of June 30, 2025, was US$43.9 billion (December 31, 2024: 49.2 billion U.S. dollars). insertions.translation_zh: Downward pressure continues as oversupply continues to have an adverse impact on rents and capital values. With Valuations are revalued in line with our current practice and the collateral buffer is reduced. This results in 2025 The level of expected credit loss provisions for the half year has increased, especially for the "credit impaired" portfolio. Anticipation letter The increase in loan loss provisions was also affected by continued adverse changes and model changes. Although it is fragrant recently The reduction in HIBOR should provide short-term liquidity for industry borrowers and improve debt repayment capabilities. However, pressure on real estate prices is likely to persist until economic conditions and market sentiment improve. because Therefore, credit changes are expected to further intensify in the second half of 2025. We continue to carefully assess and manage portfolio risks through portfolio assessments and stress testing. crisp Weak borrowers, including those with challenging debt repayment capabilities and those with high loan-to-valuation ratios People are subject to closer supervision and management.

We closely monitor our commercial real estate portfolio and assessments may reflect potential refinancing Symptoms of a problem.
